mainmatter.p: A project to explore the category theory of duploids and their higher-dimensional generalizations.

frontmatter.title: Constructive domain theoretic models of linear type theory

mainmatter.p: A project to explore the category theory of duploids and their higher-dimensional generalizations.

frontmatter.title: 2023-10-05 / higher duploid theory: “shut up and calculate”

mainmatter.p: Meeting with Fosco Loregian and Daniele Palombi. Fosco Loregian has suggested that we should tackle our question by thinking of duploids as adjunctions; then we are trying to find an adjunction-theoretic universal property for the “equalizing” completion \tilde{F} \dashv \tilde{G} of an adjunction F \dashv G.

Let F \dashv G \colon D \to C be an adjunction, and let T : \equiv GF \colon C \to C and S : \equiv FG \colon D \to D be the corresponding monad and comonad respectively. Then we have a new adjunction G' \dashv F' \colon C_T \to D_S respectively.

Let H be the collage of the distributor \mathcal{O} _{ F \dashv G } \colon C ^{ \mathsf{o} } \times D \to \mathbf{Set} of oblique maps specified as \mathcal{O} _{ F \dashv G } { \left ( P,N \right )} : \equiv C { \left ( P,GN \right )}, so that we have:
mainmatter.tex: \begin{aligned} H { \left ( P,Q \right )} &= C { \left ( P,Q \right )} \\ H { \left ( M,N \right )} &= D { \left ( M,N \right )} \\ H { \left ( P,N \right )} &= \mathcal{O} _{ F \dashv G } { \left ( P,N \right )} = C { \left ( P,GN \right )} \\ H { \left ( N,P \right )} &= \varnothing \end{aligned}
mainmatter.p: Let K be the collage of the distributor \mathcal{O} _{ G' \dashv F' } \colon D_S ^{ \mathsf{o} } \times C_T \to \mathbf{Set} specified as \mathcal{O} _{ G' \dashv F' } { \left ( N,P \right )} : \equiv D_S { \left ( N,F'P \right )}, so that we have:
The initial conjecture of Fosco Loregian is that we can form some kind of square like the following, in which the diagonal map is the unit of the equalizing completion of an adjunction:
It is unclear if the square above is supposed to commute fully or only laxly. But if something happens here, maybe there is a universal property.

Met with Fosco Loregian and Daniele Palombi on higher duploid theory. Fosco Loregian suggested that the immediate goal is to find a proper universal property for the Munch–Maccagnoni’s duploid construction as some kind of gluing or pushout, as it looks tantalizingly similar to a collage — it is almost the collage of the profunctor of a given adjunction, except that in the place where you usually have no morphisms, there are morphisms.

The obvious obstruction to a category-theoretic universal property is that it starts with categories and yields a non-category except in degenerate cases, but the idea of Fosco Loregian is that we might consider a duploid-theoretic universal property! As a category is a degenerate kind of duploid, we might consider the duploid construction as in fact happening in the category of duploids. This is not entirely nonsense, as duploids are models of an essentially algebraic theory and so can in principle be glued together.

Fosco Loregian has tried some naïve possibilities for such a gluing — but so far these have not worked. We concluded that we need to have a “shut up and calculate” meeting in order to actually come up with a conjecture as to what the gluing needs to be. We have set this meeting for 2023-10-05.

We characterise the polarised evaluation order through a categorical structure where the hypothesis that composition is associative is relaxed. Duploid is the name of the structure, as a reference to Jean-Louis Loday’s duplicial algebras. The main result is a reflection \textit{Adj} \to \textit{Dupl} where \textit{Dupl} is a category of duploids and duploid functors, and \textit{Adj} is the category of adjunctions and pseudo maps of adjunctions. The result suggests that the various biases in denotational semantics: indirect, call-by-value, call-by-name… are a way of hiding the fact that composition is not always associative.
